2604.08384.title: TASU2: Controllable CTC Simulation for Alignment and Low-Resource Adaptation of Speech LLMs
2604.08384.paper_summary: Speech LLM post-training increasingly relies on efficient cross-modal alignment and robust low-resource adaptation, yet collecting large-scale audio-text pairs remains costly. Text-only alignment methods such as TASU reduce this burden by simulating CTC posteriors from transcripts, but they provide limited control over uncertainty and error rate, making curriculum design largely heuristic. We propose \textbf{TASU2}, a controllable CTC simulation framework that simulates CTC posterior distributions under a specified WER range, producing text-derived supervision that better matches the acoustic decoding interface. This enables principled post-training curricula that smoothly vary supervision difficulty without TTS. Across multiple source-to-target adaptation settings, TASU2 improves in-domain and out-of-domain recognition over TASU, and consistently outperforms strong baselines including text-only fine-tuning and TTS-based augmentation, while mitigating source-domain performance degradation.
2604.08384.paper_summary_zh: 語音 LLM 的後訓練越來越依賴高效的跨模態對齊和穩健的低資源適應，然而收集大規模的音頻-文本對仍然成本高昂。僅基於文本的對齊方法如 TASU 通過從轉錄文本模擬 CTC 後驗來減輕這一負擔，但它們對不確定性和錯誤率的控制有限，使得課程設計在很大程度上是啟發式的。我們提出了 \textbf{TASU2}，一個可控的 CTC 模擬框架，在指定的 WER 範圍內模擬 CTC 後驗分佈，產生更好匹配聲學解碼介面的文本衍生監督。這使得後訓練課程能夠以原則性方式平滑變化監督難度，而無需 TTS。在多個源到目標的適應設置中，TASU2 在 TASU 的基礎上提高了域內和域外識別率，並持續超越包括僅基於文本的微調和基於 TTS 的增強等強基準，同時減輕了源域性能下降的問題。
2604.08384.author: Jing Peng et.al.
2604.08384.authors: Jing Peng, Chenghao Wang, Yi Yang, Lirong Qian, Junjie Li, Yu Xi, Shuai Wang, Kai Yu
